Commissioll 
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322-5662 
Administratian

322-6441 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Cleo Moore 
P.O. Box 7 
North San Juan, CA 
95960 
March 27, 1984 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-84-036 
Dear Ms. Moore: 
Thank you for your request for advice on the 
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
facts were provided in your letter and during my 
conversation with you on March 7, 1984: 
FACTS 
conflict of 
The following 
telephone 
On November 25, 1983, you were appointed Director of the San 
Juan Ridge County Water District. 
You resigned the position at 
the suggestion of the Nevada County Counsel's Office, which felt 
that you had a potential conflict of interest due to your 
employment by the california Green Thumb. 
The Green Thumb is a 
nonprofit organization which provides work for senior citizens. 
Your job title is "crew foreman" and your function is to oversee 
work crews. 
One of the entities for whom the Green Thumb crews 
perform work is the North San Juan Ridge County Water District. 
The crew's services for the District include ~leaning ditches of 
debris, checking for vandalism, monitoring the amount of water 
used, and handling much of the office work. 
You are paid a 
minimum wage for up to 20 hours of work per week. 
These wages 
are paid by the program, which is federally subsidized, and not 
by the entities for whom your crew work. 
Your supervisors 
arrange for the jobs that your crew performs. 
QUESTION PRESENTED 
Do you have a conflict of interest that prevents you from 
serving as Director of the San Juan Ridge County Water District? 


Cleo Moore 
March 27, 1984 
Page 2 
CONCLUSION 
The Political Reform Act does not prevent you from serving 
as the Director of the Water District. 
However, it may affect 
your governmental decisionmaking as Director. 
DISCUSSION 
The conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform 
Act do not prohibit you from serving as the Director of the 
Water District. 
However, the Act's provisions may affect your 
decisionmaking as Director. 
Government Code Section 871001/ 
prohibits a local puOlic official from making or participating 
in the making of a governmental decision in which she knows or 
has reason to know that she has a financial interest. 
An 
official has a "financial interest" in a decision within the 
meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that 
the decision will have a material financial effec~1 on: 
* * * 
(c) 
Any source of income • • • aggregating two 
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided 
to ••• the public official within 12 months prior to 
••• the decision is made •••• 
(Section 87l03(c) .) 
If, as the Director of the Water Disrict, you are confronted 
with a decision that will have a material financial effect on 
Green Thumb, you must disqualify yourself because it is a source 
of income to you. 
A decision is said to have a "material" 
effect on a nonprofit organization if the decision's effect will 
be "substantial." 
(See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
l8702(b) (3) (D).) 
If you have difficulty in determining whether 
your disqualification is required on a particular decision, you 
can contact our office for advice. 
The County Counsel's Office is concerned that, as the 
Director of the Water District, you may be confronted with 
11 
Hereinafter all statutory references are to the 
Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. 
11 
For an explanation of the phrase "material financial 
effect," see the enclosed copy 2 cal. Adm. Code Section 18702. 


f 
Cleo Moore 
Ma rch 27, 1984 
Page 3 
decisions which will affect the amount of worK and, hence, the 
amount of money, that you receive from the Green Thumb. 
Under 
the Act, you are required to disqualify yourself from decisions 
which will directly increase your income by $100 or more. 
(2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702 (b) (3) (A) .) 
This means that you 
must disqualify yourself if you know or have reason to know that 
a decision will result in your personally receiving sufficient 
additional hours of work such that your income will be increased 
by at least $100. 
My advice is confined to the provisions of the Political 
Reform Act. 
You should contact your County Counsel's Office 
concerning the possible application of Section 1090, which is 
outside the FPPC's jurisdiction. 
If you have any additional 
questions concerning the Political Reform Act, please feel free 
to contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
JSM:plh 
Enclosures 
Very truly yours, 
9~~~~ 
Janis Shank MCLea~'~ 
Counsel 
Legal Division 






COU

STATE OF CALIFORNIA

Raberl H. Wdder, 2nd District 
Karsten Hansen, 3rd Districi 
Eric W. Rood, 4th Disfrid 
Patricio S. Sutton, 5th Dislrid 
.. ...

Cleo H. Moore 
P. O. Box 7 
North San Juan, CA 95960 
Dear Mrs. Moore: 
OF NE 
ADA 
BOARD OF SUPERVISORS 
201 Church Street 
NEVADA CITY, CALIFORNIA 95959 
Phone, Area Cod. 916. ~65 • 1480 
November 30, 1983 
On October 3, 1983, the Nevada County Board of Supervisors adopted 
Resolution 83-383, authorizing appointments in lieu of election to 
special district boards. 
This action approved your appointment to 
the office of Director, San Juan Ridge County Water District, for 
a four-year term, effective November 25, 1983. 
Transmitted herewith please find a "Certificate of Appointment and 
Oath of Office" and "Statement of Economic Interest" to be completed 
and returned to this office prior to your voting as a Director and 
no later than December 25, 1983. 
The oath of office certificate 
must be executed either in the presence of a Deputy County Clerk or 
Notary Public. 
Thank you for your cooperation in this matter and congratulations 
on your appointment. 
Ict 
Very truly yours, 
~-2~/.Ld~ 
CAIHY R. THOMPSON 
Clerk of the Board
